description:
PROJECT SCOPE

Provide one new dedicated AFCI/GFCI protected 20A 120V branch circuit from the main panel on the exterior wall of the house to a new kitchen range hood.
No meter work being performed.

INSTALLATION DETAILS

Install a vertical 1-inch Steel EMT raceway exiting the main electrical panel from a 1-inch LR conduit body (located below all energized parts) on the homes exterior brick wall. Transition raceway to horizontal orientation for building penetration using a 1 LB. Penetrate into the accessible attic with 1 EMT terminating into a 4-11/16 steel junction box with a blank cover.

Install #10-2 UF-B cable (1 HOT 1 NEUTRAL 1 EGC) (Sunlight Resistant/Wet Location rated) from the range hood to the steel junction box. Cable will be routed along ceiling rafters; any portion below 7 will be protected by guard strips per NEC 320.23. The #10-2 UF-B will be sleeved continuously from the 4-11/16 junction box through the LB down the vertical EMT and through the LR terminating directly into the main panel . This avoids all splices providing a continuous run from the OCPD to the appliance.

CODE COMPLIANCE NOTES:

10 AWG conductors are specd. for this 20A circuit to account for ambient temperature correction in the unconditioned attic and sun-exposed exterior raceway. The EMT will be sealed with duct seal at the attic box and panel entry to prevent moisture migration and condensation per NEC 300.7. All cables and raceways will be supported/secured per NEC. All exterior fittings/hardware will be UL-listed rain-tight compression type. All cables and raceways will be supported and secured per NEC requirements. An insulated throat fitting or plastic bushing will be used at all EMT entry points to protect the cable jacket from abrasion. All wire terminations will be torqued to the manufacturer specifications per 2023 NEC 110.14(D)

ELECTRICAL SERVICE CHANGE REQUIRED - CODE VIOLATIONS
(EXISTING SERVICE 100A) (TRANSFORMER LOCATION UNKNOWN)

The existing meter was built around making it inaccessible to the utility company and will have to be relocated in order to pass inspection.

Relocate the meter to the northeastern wall of the home just around the corner of the existing location outside. Retrench back to the transformer for new service conductors. Install a new grounding electrode system. Install a new 200/150 load center adjacent to the new meter socket. Install a 100A/2P and run new wire to the existing load center. Install a ground bar in the existing panel to isolate the grounds and neutrals. Install a surge protection device.

Interconnect with a PV breaker in the new main distribution panel.

description:
Leaking 1/2â� cold copper water line will be abandoned under the foundation.
We will connect to the main cold water manifold and run 1/2â� pex pipe up in between floors and down to the kitchen sink. 
Also the hose bib will be connected that is behind the kitchen sink. 
The run will be a total of about 50 ft pipe will be well strapped and insulated. 
We will also replace all 13 compression angle stops at all fixtures with new quarter turn 1/2â�x3/8â� compression stops.
Hot and cold valves will be replaced at the washer box. 
Angle stops are hard to turn and potentially not stopping the water completely replacing them will also reduce the chance of future water damage.
After work is completed we will test and make sure there are no further leaks in the home. 
There will be holes cut in walls and ceilings. 
Water pressure to the house is currently at 105 psi. 
Plumbing code allows water pressure to be up to 80 psi. 
Recommend adding a PRV in front of the city meter on the homeowner side. 
We will bring down the pressure to the correct psi.
This will include excavating finding the main water line and adding the PRV. 
We will have it in a valve box and backfill around it that way it is accessible without having to dig to find it. 
-While adding the PRV also recommend adding a homeowner shut off valve. 
There is currently no easy accessible way for the homeowner to turn the water off in an emergency. 
This will also be in a valve box in front of the city meter. 
-2 toilets have running water due to fill valve failures both fill valves will be replaced and tested to make sure there are no further leaks.
